scope: Hero Effects and Utilities Initialization Flow
interaction_flow: app.js:init() → initAnchorScroll(): queries all a[href^='#'], attaches click handlers → window.scrollTo({ top: targetPos-40, behavior:'auto' })
app.js:init() → initScrollReveal(): queries [data-reveal] elements → inView(el, ()=>el.classList.add('revealed'), { margin:'-50px' }) for each
initHeroEffect → resize(): getBoundingClientRect, sets canvas.width/height * dpr, ctx.scale(dpr,dpr) → calls initGrid()
initHeroEffect:resize → initGrid(): clears points[], computes gap (40 if width<768 else 50), creates Point(x,y) for each grid cell
initHeroEffect → draw(): ctx.clearRect; forEach point → point.update() [spring physics + mouse repulsion]; draws grid lines; requestAnimationFrame(draw)
initHeroEffect → new IntersectionObserver(): observe(canvas); on not-intersecting → cancelAnimationFrame; on intersecting → draw()
initHeroShader → new ShaderMount(container, meshGradientFragmentShader, uniforms, undefined, speed=1.0): starts WebGL mesh gradient animation
GlassTerminal/LensEffect → initSplitCompare(container, options): queries .split-container, .split-after, .split-divider; if any missing → return null
initSplitCompare → recalcSkewOffset(): getBoundingClientRect on splitContainer, computes skewOffset from tan(angle)*height/width
initSplitCompare → updateSplit(defaultPosition=70): sets clipPath on .split-after, sets .split-divider left%
On mousemove/touchmove → targetX updated → startAnimation() → requestAnimationFrame(animate) → lerp currentX→targetX → updateSplit(currentX)
On threshold crossing (defaultPosition > animationThreshold=40) → retriggerAnimations(): clone/replace .split-content or reflow CSS animations
initThemeToggle → applyTheme(storedTheme): html.classList.remove('light','dark'); add class if 'light' or 'dark'; update .theme-toggle-btn active states
initThemeToggle → toggle.addEventListener('click'): on .theme-toggle-btn click → setStoredTheme(theme) [localStorage.setItem] → applyTheme(theme)
initThemeToggle → window.matchMedia('prefers-color-scheme: dark').addEventListener('change'): if stored==='system' or null → applyTheme('system')
key_relationships: initHeroEffect owns Point class (internal) — spring physics with mouse repulsion
initSplitCompare exports initAllSplitCompare(selector) — batch init over querySelectorAll
initAnchorScroll depends_on window.scrollTo (behavior:'auto' for instant jump)
app.js:init() calls initAnchorScroll, initScrollReveal (NOT initHeroEffect/initHeroShader/initThemeToggle — those are standalone exports)
important_notes: initHeroEffect and initHeroShader are EXPORTED but NOT called from app.js:init() — intended for sub-pages or direct HTML script usage
initThemeToggle is also exported but not called from app.js:init()
Both hero effects have prefers-reduced-motion guards as first check — critical accessibility feature
initHeroEffect uses canvas 2D API; initHeroShader uses WebGL via ShaderMount
initSplitCompare is reusable (accepts container + options), used in two components
Split-compare threshold logic re-triggers CSS animations when divider crosses animationThreshold=40%
initAnchorScroll uses behavior:'auto' (instant) deliberately for better UX on long pages
initScrollReveal margin:'-50px' means elements reveal 50px before they would naturally enter viewport
